dialogues: See this sign right here? The equals sign. Here's something important about it.
It means SAME VALUE AS. 3 times 4 has the same value as 12. Not the answer is. Equals means same value.
And since both sides have the same value, we can flip them around.
12 equals 3 times 4. Same equation. Same value on both sides. Just written differently.
Both of these are correct. The equals sign works both ways, because it means same value as.
Your turn. Build the equation for these boxes.
You got it. 6 times 7 equals 42. Six boxes, 7 in each, same value as 42.
And we can also write it this way. Same value, both sides.
Same idea. Count the bags, count what's inside, figure out the product. Build the equation.
Right. 5 times 9 equals 45. Five bags, 9 in each, same value as 45.
